message.content:
Adversarial reviewer: REFUTE this candidate; default refuted=true unless solid. Read the real chia code at C:/bugBounty/targets/chia-blockchain first.

CANDIDATE: DataLayer client SSRF via attacker-controlled on-chain mirror URLs (no scheme/host allowlist)
class: SSRF (CWE-918) severity: Medium
file: chia/data_layer/download_data.py:313
source: On-chain DataLayer Mirror coin `urls` field. Anyone can create a Mirror coin for any launcher_id/store_id on-chain; it is returned by wallet_rpc.dl_get_mirrors and is fully attacker-controlled (untrusted, no auth).
sink: aiohttp ClientSession.get(server_info.url + "/" + filename, proxy=proxy_url) in http_download() at download_data.py:313-318
data_flow: DataLayer.update_subscriptions_from_wallet (data_layer.py:953) -> dl_get_mirrors returns Mirror.urls (on-chain, attacker-controlled) -> data_store.update_subscriptions_from_wallet stores them as ServerInfo.url -> DataLayer.fetch_and_validate (data_layer.py:607) reads get_available_servers_for_store and loops server_info -> insert_from_delta_file (download_data.py:171) -> download_file (download_data.py:110) with downloader=None -> http_download (download_data.py:298) -> session.get(server_info.url + '/' + filename). No validation that the URL is http(s) only, no host/IP allowlist, no block of RFC1918/localhost/link-local/metadata (169.254.169.254) targets. The fetch is a blind GET issued from the victim node.
excerpt: async with session.get(server_info.url + "/" + filename, headers=headers, timeout=timeout, proxy=proxy_url) as resp:

LENS = CONCRETE EXPLOIT. Construct the exact message/request/offer and the resulting impact. If you cannot build a plausible exploit from the real code, refuted=true.

is_real=true only if it survives against the real code. Provide exploit_sketch and adjusted_severity if real. Return ONLY the structured object.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- contribute:contribute: This skill should be used when the user asks to "contribute to open source", "find an issue to work on", "analyze a repo", "submit a pull request", "review PR feedback", "triage an issue", "sync my fork", "create a release", "clean up contribution branches", "debug CI failures", or mentions open-source contribution workflows. Provides a full-lifecycle open-source contribution system with 12 phases.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

298	async def http_download(
299	    target_filename_path: Path,
300	    filename: str,
301	    proxy_url: str | None,
302	    server_info: ServerInfo,
303	    timeout: aiohttp.ClientTimeout,
304	    log: logging.Logger,
305	    max_delta_file_size: int,
306	) -> None:
307	    """
308	    Download a file from a server using aiohttp.
309	    Raises exceptions on errors
310	    """
311	    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
312	        headers = {"accept-encoding": "gzip"}
313	        async with session.get(
314	            server_info.url + "/" + filename,
315	            headers=headers,
316	            timeout=timeout,
317	            proxy=proxy_url,
318	        ) as resp:
319	            resp.raise_for_status()
320	            size = int(resp.headers.get("content-length", 0))
321	            max_delta_file_size_bytes = max_delta_file_size * 1024 * 1024
322	            if size > max_delta_file_size_bytes:
323	                raise MaxDeltaFileSizeExceededError(f"Maximum delta file size exceeded: {max_delta_file_size} MiB.")
324	            log.debug(f"Downloading delta file {filename}. Size {size} bytes.")
325	            progress_byte = 0
326	            progress_percentage = f"{0:.0%}"
327	            success = False
328	            try:
329	                with target_filename_path.open(mode="wb") as f:
330	                    async for chunk, _ in resp.content.iter_chunks():
331	                        f.write(chunk)
332	                        progress_byte += len(chunk)
333	                        if progress_byte > max_delta_file_size_bytes:
334	                            raise MaxDeltaFileSizeExceededError(
335	                                f"Maximum delta file size exceeded: {max_delta_file_size} MiB."
336	                            )
337	                        if size > 0:
338	                            new_percentage = f"{progress_byte / size:.0%}"
339	                            if new_percentage != progress_percentage:
340	                                progress_percentage = new_percentage
341	                                log.info(f"Downloading delta file {filename}. {progress_percentage} of {size} bytes.")
342	                success = True
343	            finally:
344	                if not success:
345	                    target_filename_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
346

111	    data_store: DataStore,
112	    target_filename_path: Path,
113	    store_id: bytes32,
114	    root_hash: bytes32,

118	    downloader: PluginRemote | None,

120	    client_foldername: Path,
121	    timestamp: int,
122	    log: logging.Logger,
123	    grouped_by_store: bool,
124	    group_downloaded_files_by_store: bool,
125	    max_delta_file_size: int,
126	) -> bool:
127	    if target_filename_path.exists():
128	        return True
129	    filename = get_delta_filename(store_id, root_hash, generation, grouped_by_store)
130	
131	    if downloader is None:
132	        # use http downloader - this raises on any error
133	        try:
134	            await http_download(
135	                target_filename_path, filename, proxy_url, server_info, timeout, log, max_delta_file_size
136	            )
137	        except (asyncio.TimeoutError, aiohttp.ClientError, MaxDeltaFileSizeExceededError):
138	            new_server_info = await data_store.server_misses_file(store_id, server_info, timestamp)
139	            log.info(
140	                f"Failed to download {filename} from {new_server_info.url}."
141	                f"Miss {new_server_info.num_consecutive_failures}."
142	            )
143	            log.info(f"Next attempt from {new_server_info.url} in {new_server_info.ignore_till - timestamp}s.")
144	            return False
145	        return True
146	
147	    log.info(f"Using downloader {downloader} for store {store_id.hex()}.")
148	    request_json = {
149	        "url": server_info.url,
150	        "client_folder": str(client_foldername),
151	        "filename": filename,
152	        "group_files_by_store": group_downloaded_files_by_store,
153	        "max_delta_file_size": max_delta_file_size,
154	    }
155	    try:
156	        async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
157	            async with session.post(
158	                downloader.url + "/download",
159	                json=request_json,
160	                headers=downloader.headers,
161	                timeout=timeout,
162	            ) as response:
163	                res_json = await response.json()
164	                assert isinstance(res_json["downloaded"], bool)
165	                return res_json["downloaded"]
166	    except (asyncio.TimeoutError, aiohttp.ClientError) as e:
167	        log.error(f"download_file could not get response from plugin {downloader}: {type(e).__name__}: {e}")
168	        return False

content.text:
The full automatic loop is confirmed: `periodically_manage_data` (runs every `manage_data_interval`, default 60s) -> `update_subscription` worker -> `update_subscriptions_from_wallet` (pulls on-chain mirror URLs) -> `fetch_and_validate` -> `insert_from_delta_file` -> `download_file` -> `http_download`. This happens for every subscription the node has, with no user interaction.

Now let me verify the Mirror coin model and whether anyone can create a mirror for any launcher_id, plus check the `Mirror.urls` field type (whether it's validated as a URL anywhere). Let me also check the wallet side that creates/parses mirror coins.
